message:
Add configurable help verbosity for CLI and TUI dashboard

Implement bd-352n: allow users to configure help verbosity via config
file so they can prefer minimal or full help by default.

Config changes (config.go):
- Add HelpVerbosity field to Config struct (minimal|full, default: full)
- Add validation for help_verbosity value
- Add Print() support to output help_verbosity setting

CLI changes (root.go):
- Check config.HelpVerbosity when neither --help-minimal nor --help-full
  flags are provided
- Respects explicit flags over config preference

TUI help system (help.go):
- Add DashboardHelpVerbosity type with minimal/full constants
- Add DashboardHelpOptions struct with Verbosity and Debug fields
- Add DashboardHelpOptionsFrom() constructor
- DashboardHelpBarHints() returns context-appropriate key hints:
  - Minimal: quit, help, navigate, select (4 hints)
  - Full: adds panels, zoom, send, refresh
  - Debug: adds diag, scan, checkpoint
- DashboardHelpSections() returns context-appropriate help overlay

Dashboard integration (dashboard.go):
- Wire help verbosity from config through to help rendering
- Pass options to help bar and overlay components

This allows power users to reduce visual clutter while preserving
full help access via the ? key overlay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
